headline: Scale AI first agent baseline. Compound 2024-2026 evidence cluster: (1) DOL FLSA investigation opened August 2024 over wage theft / contractor misclassification — DROPPED May 2025 amid DOL administration change, but underlying claims unresolved; (2) 45 former Outlier/Remotasks contractors February 2025 letter to Senate Commerce Committee detailing 'wage theft' and 'widespread labor abuse' including emotional-distress claims from contractors working on Meta self-harm prevention training data; (3) two pending lawsuits (December 2024, January 2025) over misclassification and underpayment; (4) CEO Alexandr Wang named in emotional-distress lawsuit; (5) Pentagon training-data-provider role for multiple classified cohort members. Published 35.9 / Developing band — labor-rights structural pattern + Pentagon training-data role + Global South contractor concentration sustain published placement. The DOL investigation drop is INT-erosive (regulatory accountability framework structurally weakened) but does not create a band crossing. Sub-threshold drift estimated at -2.0 points; recommend confirm at 35.9.
key_evidence: August 2024: US Department of Labor opened FLSA investigation into Scale AI over wage theft and contractor-misclassification claims at Outlier/Remotasks subsidiaries.

February 2025: 45 former Scale AI contractors wrote to Senate Commerce Committee detailing 'wage theft' and 'widespread labor abuse'; emotional-distress claims from Meta self-harm prevention training-data work.
May 2025: DOL dropped investigation amid administration change; underlying claims unresolved (Inc., TechCrunch reporting).
December 2024 + January 2025: Two lawsuits filed by former workers alleging underpayment and misclassification as contractors instead of employees, denying overtime and sick-day protections.
CEO Alexandr Wang named in emotional-distress lawsuit (Inc. Magazine reporting).
Scale AI provides training data to multiple Pentagon classified cohort members — systemic data-labor role in national-security-AI supply chain.
Outlier/Remotasks contractor base concentrated in Global South (Philippines, Kenya, India) — distributional-equity dimension.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Scale AI is being investigated by the US Department of Labor
evidence.finding: DOL FLSA investigation opened August 2024 over wage theft / contractor misclassification at Scale AI subsidiaries.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — DOL dropped Scale AI investigation
evidence.finding: DOL scrapped investigation in May 2025; underlying claims unresolved; reflects DOL administration-change rollback of contractor-classification rule.

evidence.source: Inc. Magazine — Scale AI contractors allege wage theft in letter to Senators
evidence.finding: 45 former contractors detail 'wage theft' and 'widespread labor abuse' including emotional-distress claims from Meta self-harm prevention training-data work.

evidence.source: Inc. Magazine — Scale AI lawsuit Alexandr Wang
evidence.finding: CEO Alexandr Wang named in emotional-distress lawsuit.

evidence.source: Inc. Magazine — Labor Department Scraps Investigation Into Scale AI
evidence.finding: Subpoena will not be enforced post-investigation drop; structural-accountability gap documented.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 confirmed. A1 detection partially functional via media reporting and Senate inquiry; A4 amplification compromised — contractor-harm signals reach Scale AI only via litigation or Senate letter.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 2.5 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: Scale AI training-data work on self-harm prevention without contractor emotional-support framework demonstrates partial empathic-regard gap; published mid-developing placement consistent.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 2.5 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: contractor-classification stance asserted as contractor-status, denying overtime/sick-day; AC4 response reactive (post-litigation, post-Senate-letter).
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2.0 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: Outlier/Remotasks contractor base concentrated in Global South; bulk of annotation work done by lower-paid contractors while CEO/leadership US-based.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: contractor-classification policy maintained despite litigation and Senate inquiry; B5 consent: contractors allege opaque pay-per-task structures.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 confirmed. AB2 transparency: Outlier/Remotasks operations not publicly transparent; AB4 reparation: lawsuit settlements undisclosed; DOL-investigation drop weakens reparative framework.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: contractor-misclassification structure reproduces across subsidiaries; Pentagon training-data-provider role amplifies systemic significance.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2.5 confirmed. I1 consistency: 'AI for everyone' marketing posture contradicted by contractor-labor pattern; I2 non-performance: gap between brand and operational reality is sustained but DOL-investigation drop is INT-erosive (sub-threshold).

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction confirms 35.9. Sub-threshold drift estimated at -2.0 points (DOL-investigation-drop INT erosion); confirm at 35.9.

negativeSignals: DOL FLSA investigation (dropped May 2025); 45-contractor Senate Commerce letter alleging wage theft and labor abuse; CEO Alexandr Wang named in emotional-distress lawsuit; Pentagon training-data role; Global South contractor concentration; contractor-misclassification policy sustained; DOL-investigation drop reduces external accountability.
positiveSignals: DOL investigation existed (regulatory accountability functioned, even if dropped); Senate Commerce Committee letter received Senate attention; lawsuits proceeding (judicial accountability functional); $14B+ valuation provides resource base for potential remediation; recent blog/communication on contractor wellbeing programs (limited).
sector_context: AI Labs / data-labor cluster. Cross-reference: Outlier (subsidiary), Remotasks (subsidiary), Sama (predecessor), CrowdAI. Scale AI distinguished by Pentagon training-data role + CEO Wang public profile + scale of Outlier/Remotasks contractor base. Math-hygiene flag potential for adjacent labor-data entries.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with developing-band placement
DOL investigation drop registered as INT-erosive sub-threshold drift
open_actions_carried: Watch: any new labor-rights enforcement action under FLSA or state labor codes
Watch: any Pentagon training-data-related harm event surfacing
